title: Livy Method Day 32 - Spring 2025
summary: Gina Livy's Facebook Lives from The Livy Method Spring 2025 Support Group hosted on Facebook. This is a recording of the Day 32, 9 AM live. You can find the full video hosted at: www.facebook.com/groups/livymethodspring2025 In this episode, Gina unpacks why our relationship with food goes far beyond hungerвАФit's rooted in celebration, coping, and survival. She highlights the mental and emotional layers that often get overlooked, sharing why weight gain has more to do with lifeвАЩs curveballs than a lack of willpower. Gina explains how downsizing isn't about restriction, but about understanding your mindset around portions. title: Chef Andre Rush: Cooking, Combat, and Cause- A Passion for Helping Veterans and Youth
summary: Chef Andre Rush is an esteemed White House chef, U.S. Army combat veteran, and passionate mental health advocate. Known for his viral 22 push-ups a day campaign spotlighting veteran suicide awareness, Chef Rush combines military discipline with culinary artistry and a heart for service. Having turned adversity into purpose, heвАЩs been recognized with countless accoladesвАФfrom the Veterans Hall of Fame induction to national honors for suicide prevention. His lifeвАЩs mission transcends the kitchen, inspiring millions to keep going, never give up, and support one anotherвАФwhether in the military, through tragedy, or in everyday life.  ¬†  Takeaways:  Purpose Through Service: Chef Rush emphasizes that his drive comes from having вАЬno choiceвАЭ but to serveвАФturning pain and loss into a relentless mission to uplift others and raise mental health awareness.  The Power of Checking In: Both the host and Chef Rush highlight the importance of simply reaching out, reminding listeners that small gesturesвАФlike a text or a callвАФcan mean everything to someone struggling.  Redefining Strength: Chef RushвАЩs story proves that true strength isnвАЩt just physical; itвАЩs found in resilience, perseverance, and being vulnerable enough to seek help and lift others up.  ¬†  Sound Bites:  вАЬKeep going. Never give up. You can do anything.вАЭ  вАЬInstead of saying survivorвАЩs remorse, itвАЩs survivorвАЩs striveвАФitвАЩs a remembrance to remind everyone that life is short.вАЭ  вАЬYou donвАЩt have to be the person going through it to help. title: What QTR Taught Me About Time, Peace, and Power
summary: What if your life had an expiration date printed on itвАФand you just couldnвАЩt see it? This episode is a wake-up call. Not about chasing more, but about making what we already have count. I introduce a concept thatвАЩs reshaping how I live:¬†Quality Time Remaining. ItвАЩs not about how long we liveвАФitвАЩs about how we spend the precious moments we have left. I've been on this group text with some close friendsвАФsuccessful, driven guysвАФand weвАЩve started sending each other snapshots of real-life moments that matter. The ones that count. And I realized, I havenвАЩt sent one yet. That hit me. We grind so hard for the future that we forget to live today. The trouble is, we think we have time. We plan, we postpone, we sacrifice the now for the somedayвАФand sometimes, that someday never comes. IвАЩve had health scares, lost people I love, and seen too many вАЬsomedaysвАЭ stolen by surprise. ThatвАЩs why this message matters. It's not about slowing downвАФitвАЩs about becoming intentional with the time that really counts. YouвАЩll hear me share storiesвАФsome personal, some painful, all real. This isnвАЩt a theory or a mindset hack. ItвАЩs a shift in perspective that could literally change how you live. If youвАЩre waiting for the right time to make memories, itвАЩs already here. Start your¬†shorter list. Not your bucket listвАФthe little moments that matter now. Whether it's a walk, a conversation, a game with your kidвА¶ theyвАЩre priceless. The takeaway is simple: your life isnвАЩt just measured in years. ItвАЩs measured in memories. The people around you deserve more than just your timeвАФthey deserve the best of you, fully present. So do you. And if youвАЩre the kind of person whoвАЩs been grinding, achieving, and waiting for laterвА¶ itвАЩs time to wake up. Later might not come. The milk of your life tastes better now than it ever will. Savor it. Key Takeaways:   How to reframe your life around¬†Quality Time Remaining   Why being busy can rob you of what truly matters   The difference between¬†intentional living¬†and¬†life drifting   How to start your own вАЬshorter listвАЭ of moments that matter   Why presence, not productivity, is what creates lasting memories You have more time today than you might tomorrowвАФspend it like it matters. Because it does. ¬† рЯСЙ SUBSCRIBE TO ED'S YOUTUBE CHANNEL NOW рЯСИ¬†¬† вЖТ вЖТ вЖТ CONNECT WITH ED MYLETT ON SOCIAL MEDIA: вЖР вЖР вЖР¬† вЮ°пЄП INSTAGRAM¬†¬† вЮ°пЄПFACEBOOK¬†¬† вЮ°пЄП LINKEDIN¬†¬† вЮ°пЄП X¬†¬† вЮ°пЄП WEBSITE¬† ¬† ¬† Get my exclusive Monday Motivation training in GrowthDay, the worldвАЩs #1 app for advanced mindset and personal development. title: How to Get What You Want Every Time: 3 Steps to Negotiate Anything With Anyone
summary: Right now, thereвАЩs something you want.¬† Maybe you need help at home, more support from your partner, or itвАЩs time to set a boundary at work.¬† But youвАЩre avoiding the conversation. Why? Because it feels hard. You donвАЩt want conflict, you donвАЩt want to upset anyone, and you just donвАЩt know how to bring it up.¬† That ends today.¬† The greatest things in life are on the other side of hard conversations.¬† And in this episode, you will learn the 3 simple steps to have any difficult conversation and get exactly what you want.¬† Mel sits down with world renowned negotiation expert Kwame Christian, CEO of the American Negotiation Institute, to break down exactly how to approach the conversations youвАЩve been avoiding.¬† Whether you want to negotiate a raise, ask your partner to step up, or finally tell your friend whatвАЩs bothering you, this episode is your crash course in how to advocate for yourself.¬† Inside this episode, youвАЩll learn: -Why avoiding hard conversations is damaging your relationships (and your self-respect)¬† -How to handle someoneвАЩs emotional reaction without losing your cool¬† -The 3-step вАЬCompassionate CuriosityвАЭ framework to navigate ANY conflict¬† -Why itвАЩs more important to be respected than liked (and how to make that shift)¬† -How to negotiate at home, at work, and even with your landlord -How to stand up for yourself without starting a fight¬† If youвАЩve been staying silent, stewing in resentment, or dreading confrontation, this episode will give you the confidence and skills to speak up and change your life.¬† You deserve respect. title: When You Stop Being Available, Everything Changes
summary: Have you ever noticed how people treat you when you're always available versus when you set boundaries? From gaining respect to avoiding emotional labor, find out how setting healthy limits can transform your relationships and improve your overall well-being. title: Forgiveness: How to Let Go, Without Letting Them Off the Hook with Rachael Coopes
summary: What if forgiveness didnвАЩt mean forgetting, reconciling, or letting people off the hook вАФ but reclaiming your future?  Learning how to forgive - even when it feels like the hardest thing in the world to do is transformative.¬†    It is a complex process though & thereвАЩs a real art to balancing boundaries & letting go.    ItвАЩs an art Rachel Coopes is obsessed with.¬†SheвАЩs the author of The Art of Forgiveness.  рЯТ° YouвАЩll hear:  вАУ The origin of the word forgiveness вАФ and why it changes everything  вАУ The science behind rumination, stress, and long-term health  вАУ What forgiveness has to do with resilience and agency  вАУ How to begin (even if youвАЩre not ready yet)  вАУ Why boundaries are essential вАФ not the opposite of forgiveness  вАУ Why reconciliation and forgiveness are not the same  вАУ How your future can be your act of forgiveness  рЯУЦ Chapters  00:00 The Quote That Changed Everything  02:18 How Forgiveness Became RachaelвАЩs Obsession  05:10 What 25 Interviews Taught Her  06:30 Forgiveness vs Letting People Off the Hook  10:45 Forgiveness вЙ† Reconciliation  13:05 The Neuroscience of Resentment  17:55 Where to Start When Forgiveness Feels Impossible  22:00 Boundaries, Distance & Letting Go  24:45 When Forgiveness Just IsnвАЩt Possible  26:10 Resilience, Accountability & RachaelвАЩs SonвАЩs Story  29:40 Your Future as the Ultimate Act of Forgiveness  31:15 вАЬForgive Like a Grain of SandвАЭ  рЯФЧ Helpful Links:  вАУ The Art of Forgiveness by Rachael Coopes  вАУ RachaelвАЩs official website  вАУ Follow Rachael on Instagram  вАУ Watch RachaelвАЩs forgiveness interview on YouTube  рЯТМ Related episodes:  вАУ The Science of Habits that Actually Stick with Dr Bree Hurn  вАУ How to Hack your Dopamine with Dr Anastasia Hronis    рЯУЇ Watch & Subscribe:  вАУ Watch this episode on YouTube  вАУ Subscribe to the Substack  рЯУђ Get in touch:  thatshelpful @ edstott.com  Follow ThatвАЩs Helpful on Instagram

title: How Affirmations Fuel Your Manifestation Efforts
podcast_name: All Up In Your Biz with Yvonne Tchrakian
summary: In this solo episode, Yvonne talks a good bit about affirmations, explaining why they are so good for you, how to use them effectively and why using the wrong affirmations for you is counterproductive. She shares a technique to help you to build up your ability to get the most out of your affirmation efforts.  Yvonne also answers more of the questions posed by the members of her Manifestie Bestie Club and community. Including, how to keep things in perspective when what you are manifesting does not happen as fast or in the way you imagined it would, and the impact belief and desire have on manifestation.  KEY TAKEAWAYS   The effectiveness of anything you do builds the more you do it. When you do affirmations and gratitude every day, over time, you get far better results. When you tell yourself something good is going to happen to me today, it raises your vibe. Don¬іt get disheartened. Sometimes the something good that has happened to you that day is quite small, which means you¬іve not realised or fully registered the fact you¬іve got it, e.g. a free coffee. The better your vibrational state is, the easier it is to manifest. If your vibration is low nothing is going to happen. You need to let go. Truly believe something good is going to happen, not worry about how or what form it will be in and just let it happen.¬† The stronger the why, the more likely you are to achieve your goal/get what you want. Saying affirmations that you don¬іt really believe in is counterproductive. Yvonne explains why and how to get past this issue.  BEST MOMENTS  вАЬAffirmations have changed my life.вАЭ  вАЬI now do affirmations and gratitude every single day, and they are an absolute game changer.вАЭ  вАЬYou have to be at the vibrational frequency to get it.вАЭ  вАЬAre you really in a good vibrational state, or are you faking it and absolutely forcing it to happen on that day?вАЭ  вАЬYou're going to be able to manifest it if you believe and if you have the ability to surrender and let go and trust the timing of your life.вАЭ  VALUABLE RESOURCES  website: pausepenny.com  Instagram: @yvonnetchrakian_manifest @pause.penny  LinkedIn:¬†https://www.linkedin.com/in/yvonne-tchrakian-64257619/  HOST BIO  Yvonne started her career as a corporate lawyer for one of Ireland's top law firms. After the loss of a baby during pregnancy, Yvonne founded Pause. Penny - which was created to inspire people to manifest their dream lives. She is also a mom of four. Yvonne is a serial manifester, manifesting everything from her dream jobs (yes, multiple), her soulmate, dream house and even her twins!  This Podcast has been brought to you by¬†Disruptive Media. https://disruptivemedia.co.uk/

title: #140 Dr Michael Gervais - Building a Super Bowl Championship Team at Seattle Seahawks and Doing it Again with Las Vegas Raiders
summary: Dr. Michael Gervais is one of the worldвАЩs leading high-performance psychologists with an obsession about the relationship between the mind and human performance. His clients include the US Olympic beach volleyball team, Olympic athletes, NBA players, Formula 1 racing, Felix Baumgartner (jumping from space as part of Red Bull Stratos) and for 9 years he partnered with Coach Pete Carroll at the Seattle Seahawks, helping them to an NFL Superbowl Championship in 2014.¬† MichaelвАЩs Business, Finding Mastery, consults to Fortune 500 CEOs and leading organisations. As host of the Finding Mastery Podcast, Michael takes you inside the minds of the worldвАЩs most extraordinary thinkers and doers. And on a personal note, Michael has been extremely generous with his time, mentoring Jon Clarke (JC) and I behind the scenes with some of the frameworks we are introducing with the Manly Sea Eagles. JC joined me behind the mic for this rich, robust and raw discussion about all things performance psychology and stepping up when it matters.¬† In this episode Andrew, JC and Dr Michael discuss: 2:30 How Michael feels about his bio being read out, growing up being obsessed with wanting to be better and feeling very grateful for where he is now. 6:00 How Michael was hit with imposter syndrome before going on stage at a global conference, how he managed to calm himself down through a form of mindfulness, and how that has led to his 27 year long mindfulness and presence practise. 12:50 "Did I do my best to prepare for this moment?" When something really matters, you do what it takes to make it happen and how Michael leveraged some parts of his sometimes challenging upbringing. 18:20 MichaelвАЩs purpose is unlocking the potential that lies dormant within people, how to unlock your personal purpose and its cousins (vision and values) and the importance of having clarity. ¬†24:05 Hacks are for hacks, confidence in your skills takes time to grow, and working with the head coach of the Seattle Seahawks, Pete Carrol, for 9 years. 31:55 Why Michael loves working in sport, avoiding having corridor conversations, and the 3 rules that kept the culture going at Seattle Seahawks. 39:15 Releasing the magic of players and how good coaching is as simple as letting someone know what they did right and speaking the language of high performance "that, yes that!" 44:35 The 3 types of skills to get better at, the learnings from losing a Super Bowl, better preparing players and teams to deal with loss, and the "longbody" concept rooted in that Native American tradition of tribal members being connected. 53:50 Vulnerability and courage are cousins, what JC is afraid of, and MichaelвАЩs story about saving his marriage with intensive therapy and working on himself. 1:07:15 Why Andrew loves working in the mental skills field and why JC loves working in sport.¬† You can find Dr Michael at his LinkedIn: title: #264: Another Way to Understand It

title: #116 - YNAB's Future Vision with CEO Todd Curtis
summary: In today's episode Ben and Ernie talk with the man himself, Todd Curtis, long-time YNAB team member and CEO since 2021. Todd has been a YNAB'er going all the way back to the YNAB3 days, and served as a beta tester for an early Mac version of the software before officially joining the company in 2010. In his tenure at YNAB Todd has touched on just about every aspect of the business, from customer service to product development, and now leads the company and helps shape and focus the core values of spendfulness that YNAB stands for.  ¬†  In this episode, Todd discusses his history with YNAB and how a fateful email to Jesse led to him getting a job. He talks about the evolution in philosophy from budgeting to planning, the five questions, and how some recently released features on the YNAB app aim to guide new users to plan their money without having to reference supporting materials. Todd also discusses the future vision of the company, and how he sees the app and the method evolving to better support YNAB's core mission to teach people to live more spendfully.  ¬†  Follow Budget Nerds on YouTube  Budget Nerds Livestreams: https://www.youtube.com/@YNABofficial/streams  Budget Nerds Episodes: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCuIUGmbCDklkDCDm-cQqv2g  ¬†  Share your YNAB wins with Ben and Ernie!  budgetnerds@ynab.com  ¬†  Get your tickets to YNAB Fan Fest 2025! Coming to San Diego, Minneapolis, and NYC. Tickets are ON SALE NOW:  YNAB.com/events

title: 442: Regulation is the New Discipline
summary: Emotional Regulation: The New Discipline for a Chaotic World   Emotional regulation has become an essential skill for personal growth and success, especially in today's fast-paced and often chaotic world. The ability to manage one's emotional responses, rather than attempting to control external circumstances, is crucial for effectively navigating life's challenges.   Tonya Leigh explores the crucial concept of emotional regulation in today's fast-paced and divisive world. Tonya emphasizes that instead of trying to control external circumstances, our true power lies in managing our emotional responses. She begins with a personal check-in, encouraging listeners to assess their feelings on a scale from zero to ten.   Despite facing significant challenges, including her father's illness and business setbacks, Tonya shares how her commitment to self-work has helped her maintain a positive outlook, rating her emotional state as a nine. The episode highlights the importance of nurturing our nervous systems and offers insights into overcoming fear of the future.   Tune in for valuable strategies on how to cultivate emotional resilience and thrive in turbulent times.   Talking Points:     00:44 - Self-Check: How Are You Feeling?  02:09 - Fear of the Future and Self-Image  04:45 - Understanding Self-Image and State of Being  08:42 - Subconscious Blocks and Self-Image  11:51 - External Control vs. Internal Regulation  15:01 - Self-Soothing and Mothering Yourself  18:44 - Regulation in Entrepreneurship  22:42 - New You, New Nervous System  23:14 - Join the School of Self-Image     Quotes:  "Regulation is the new discipline because we are living in some wild times."  "If you don't decide on purpose, if you don't take ownership of that, you are going to have a very dysregulated nervous system."  "A lot of us are just walking around as children in adult bodies."  "When you start becoming this person for yourself, you will be amazed at what you're capable of."  ¬†"I'm going to be the one that changes. That's when miracles happen."  "When you can regulate your nervous system and you don't freak out, when you have that ability to just take a pause, go in, take some breaths, calm yourself down, remind yourself, I'm safe in this moment."  Useful¬† Resources:  Click HERE to join the Membership¬†  Click HERE for a FREE download¬†  Click HERE to sign up for our newsletter, The Edit  ¬†  Connect with Self-Image Coach Tonya Leigh:  Click HERE to follow our Instagram  Click HERE to visit our website  Click HERE to visit our Facebook group  Click HERE to follow our TikTok  Click HERE to subscribe to our YouTube channel

title: Two Worlds, One Heart: Navigating Belief Systems & Relationships as an Immigrant
summary: Send us a text What happens when your heart belongs to two worlds? In this deeply personal and reflective episode, Dr. Slava peels back the emotional and cultural layers of what it really means to be an immigrant navigating life between your "home world" values and the expectations of a "new world" society. From gender roles and generational pressures to faith, family, and freedomвАФthis episode speaks directly to the internal tug-of-war many immigrants silently endure. Dr. Slava explores: рЯТФ The emotional cost of living between two culturesрЯІ† The mindset shifts required to reconcile identity without losing authenticityрЯТђ How language, gender dynamics, and unspoken expectations shape immigrant relationshipsрЯТЮ What it takes to build and sustain a strong relationship in a new world without abandoning your rootsWhether youвАЩre an immigrant, a first-generation trailblazer, or simply someone navigating love and life across cultural linesвАФthis episode is a must-listen. title: Why Your Brain Craves Revenge and How to Break Free
podcast_name: Getting Better with Jonathan Van Ness
summary: Revenge, why do we want to get revenge so bad? And where does that urge come from? And how do we go from wanting to get revenge to being able to forgive someone or others? James Kimmel Jr., Yale lecturer and co-founder of the Yale Collaborative for Motive Studies, is on Getting Better this week to teach us just that. James gives us insight into the psychology of these complex emotions, the parts of the brain affected, and what research, studies and science shows us about forgiveness. James Kimmel, Jr., JD, is a lecturer in psychiatry at the Yale School of Medicine, the founder and co-director of the Yale Collaborative for Motive Control Studies, and a researcher, lawyer, and novelist who focuses on neuroscience, psychology, revenge, addiction, forgiveness, and violence. A breakthrough scholar and expert on revenge and forgiveness, he first identified compulsive revenge seeking as an addiction and first developed the behavioral addiction model of revenge and the brain disease model of revenge addiction as public health approaches for preventing and treating violence. More can be found on his personal website, www.jameskimmeljr.com. Full Video Episodes now available on YouTube. Not A Phase. Trans Lifeline Follow us on Instagram @gettingbetterwithjvn Jonathan on Instagram @jvn Senior Producer, Chris McClure Producer, Editor & Engineer is Nathanael McClure Production support from Julie Carrillo, Anne Currie, and Chad Hall Our theme music is also composed by Nathanael McClure. summary: Do you ever find yourself holding back when you invest in training because youвАЩre worried about wasting time or money? That fear can quietly limit how much you get from any experience. title: LATE DIAGNOSED ADHD: The hidden cost of going undiagnosed for most of your life
summary: вАЬFor most of my life, I thought I was just a bad person вАФ lazy, dramatic, messy, and too muchвАЭ. In this episode of LATE BLOOMERS, Rox shares the raw truth about being diagnosed with ADHD in adulthood, and how it changed everything. Alongside Rich, she reflects on the years of masking, people-pleasing, addiction, and breakdowns that came from not understanding her own brain. Together, they explore the emotional weight of a late diagnosis вАФ the grief, the relief, the shame, and the transformation. If you've ever asked yourself вАЬWhy am I like this?вАЭ вАФ if youвАЩve ever felt like youвАЩre failing at life for reasons you canвАЩt explain вАФ this episode will hit home. Because sometimes, a diagnosis isnвАЩt just a label. ItвАЩs a lifeline.

title: What is a normal period? With Dr. Lara Briden
summary: Today, we are revisiting another conversation with Dr. Lara Briden. In this episode, Lara joins us to break down what a normal period actually looks likeвАФbecause letвАЩs be honest, most of us were never taught this. We cover how long your period should last, how much blood loss is normal, and what symptoms might signal a hormonal imbalance. A big takeaway? Having a period doesnвАЩt always mean youвАЩre ovulating. If your cycles are long, irregular, or your period lasts more than seven days, you might not be producing enough progesterone, which plays a huge role in balancing hormones, mood, and metabolism. We also discuss heavy periods, post-pill cycles, and why your period is a key health indicatorвАФplus, how low progesterone can impact anxiety, energy, and PMS.¬† If youвАЩve ever wondered whether your period is normal, this episode is for you! title: The 6 Hidden Burnout Patterns Keeping High-Achieving Women Stuck
summary: Burnout doesn't always look like collapse. For ambitious women, it often manifests as perfectionism, over-functioning, or being the one who holds everything together while slowly eroding from within. Despite countless late-night Google searches and implementing generic self-care advice, nothing seems to stickвАФbecause you're facing a unique form of burnout that requires tailored solutions. This episode reveals the groundbreaking concept of six distinct burnout archetypes that I've identified after working with hundreds of women. We explore the profound difference between visible burnout (the physical collapse) and invisible burnout (the over-functioning that keeps you going while slowly depleting your essence). You'll discover whether you're a Silent Martyr sacrificing yourself with resentment, a Perfect Striver driven by unworthiness, a Relentless Juggler managing everything with military precision, a Self-Help Scholar who knows all the tools yet remains stuck, a Hollow High Achiever successful yet numb inside, or an Untapped Powerhouse frustrated by playing small despite your enormous potential. What makes this framework revolutionary is how it addresses the unique patterns driving your specific burnout type. Unlike generic "bro advice" that ignores the complex realities of women's lives and responsibilities, understanding your archetype provides the key to breaking free from exhausting cycles. These patterns aren't who you truly areвАФthey're how you've been conditioned to function in the world. By identifying your unique burnout profile, you can finally implement strategies that work for your specific situation, reclaim your time and energy, and create success on your terms without sacrificing yourself in the process. title: Ep 23 - Breaking Up with Diet Culture (Spoiler: ItвАЩs Toxic AF) with Lotta Dann
summary: In this refreshingly raw and witty episode, Lotta DannвАФauthor, sobriety advocate, and anti-diet warriorвАФtalks about the tangled web of body image, addiction, and freedom from toxic cultural norms. Lotta unpacks the truth behind diet culture and shares her own vulnerable journey from extreme dieting to empowered body acceptance.  We discuss:   LottaвАЩs Wake-Up Call  No big bangвАФjust a slow anti-diet awakening via social media Her вАЬahaвАЭ moment: realising diet culture is a lie built on profit and control  The Addiction Crossover  How her battle with alcohol and diet culture shares common roots Emotional coping, control, and the myth of вАЬhealthyвАЭ restriction вАЬWhen self-soothing becomes self-harmвАЭвАФthe turning point  Inside Diet CultureвАЩs Dirty Lies  Diets donвАЩt workвАФmost people regain the weight (plus some!) Fat вЙ† unhealthy; health at every size is real The billion-dollar shame machine keeps us hooked  The Recovery Shift  From thigh gaps to food freedom: LottaвАЩs personal healing Learning to eat what she wantsвАФwithout guilt, without obsession Mindfulness and awareness as superpowers in recovery  Shame and the Inner Critic  A jaw-dropping reading from her journal: brutal self-talk laid bare Humour, honesty, and mindfulness to disarm the shame cycle   Practical Tools & Advice  Daily habits: challenge negative thoughts, ditch the scales, unfollow toxic accounts Morning pages (a la Julia Cameron) for mental decluttering Surround yourself with anti-diet practitioners and media Choose movement for strength, not weight loss   Standout Quotes  вАЬMy life was diminished down to two sets of scalesвАФone for my body, one for my food.вАЭ  вАЬThe pants arenвАЩt wrong for not fitting your bodyвАФyour body isnвАЩt wrong for not fitting the pants.вАЭ  вАЬDiet culture is noisyвАФbecause itвАЩs hungry.вАЭ  вАЬSobriety is a gift that keeps on giving.вАЭ  Resources & Mentions   Lotta's book: Mrs. D is Not on a Diet Website: Living Sober Movement: Health at Every Size (HAES) Book: The ArtistвАЩs Way by Julia Cameron Anti-diet dietitians and social media (found at the end of LottaвАЩs book)  Final Thought  This episode is a gentle yet fierce invitation to disentangle yourself from the shame spiral and reclaim your body, mind, and joy. Whether you've ever counted calories or counted drinks, LottaвАЩs message hits home: You are enough, just as you are.

title: Men Can Be Victims Of Narcissistic Abuse Too
summary: In this powerful episode, IвАЩm joined by Russell Tellup, a brave and deeply reflective man who opens up about the childhood abuse that shaped every corner of his life. From a young age, Russell was made to feel like the problem. Unseen. Unheard. Unloved. His sense of worth was crushed before it had a chance to form, leaving him to carry the unbearable belief that he didnвАЩt matter.As we talk, Russell shares how those early wounds followed him into adulthood, leading him into relationships with narcissistic partners who mirrored the same emotional neglect and manipulation he experienced as a child. What unfolds in this conversation is raw and real. There are moments of deep sadness. ThereвАЩs anger. And thereвАЩs a profound sense of clarity as he begins to piece together the lifelong pattern of trauma that kept him stuck as he now helps other men in this position.This is not just a story of pain, itвАЩs a story of awakening. RussellвАЩs courage to speak his truth is what transforms it. And in his vulnerability, youвАЩll find extraordinary strength.So often, we donвАЩt hear the stories of men whoвАЩve endured narcissistic abuse. But it happens. And it matters. RussellвАЩs voice cuts through the silence and reminds us that abuse doesnвАЩt discriminate, it just hides where shame lives.If youвАЩve ever felt like the black sheep, like you were never enough, or like you kept attracting the same kind of pain, you are not alone. This conversation will stay with you. RussellвАЩs story is a reminder that even in the darkest places, there is the possibility of healing, and there is always power in finally being heard.If you need to get in touch with Russell, he has a private Facebook community for men: https://www.facebook.com/share/g/1AYsbsh7uw/DonвАЩt ever be alone in your journey and make sure you come and join my private Facebook community, No Visible Bruises where you can connect with other survivors of narcissistic abuse, domestic abuse and coercive control:https://www.facebook.com/groups/novisiblebruisesDisclaimer:The views and opinions shared in this podcast are those of the individuals involved and are intended for informational and educational purposes only. They do not substitute professional or medical advice. If youвАЩve been affected by anything discussed in todayвАЩs episode, please consider reaching out to a qualified healthcare provider or mental health professional for support. YouвАЩre not alone, and help is always available

title: Productivity Smarts 102 - How To Practice Your Craft While Preserving Your Traditional Values with Steven Melman
summary: In this episode of Productivity Smarts, host Gerald J. Leonard sits down with seasoned psychotherapist and author Steven Melman, who brings over 40 years of experience in the mental health world. Their conversation dives into what it really takes to stay productive, grounded, and emotionally resilientвАФespecially for those in helping professions like therapy, coaching, and consulting.  Steven shares insights from his book, Guidance for New Clinicians in a Chaotic World: How to Practice Your Craft While Preserving Your Traditional Values. Whether youвАЩre just starting out or have years under your belt, youвАЩll find plenty of practical advice on setting boundaries, avoiding burnout, and even using humor as a powerful tool in your work.  Gerald and Steven get real about the challenges clinicians face todayвАФfrom the mental health impact of marijuana legalization to the importance of mentally вАЬclocking in and out.вАЭ They also talk about the вАЬgoal wheel,вАЭ a tool for setting and tracking goals across all areas of life, and how self-discipline plays a key role in staying on track.  If youвАЩre a clinician, coach, or just someone who wants to align your purpose with your productivity, youвАЩll walk away from this episode with fresh perspective, useful tips, and a renewed sense of direction. Tune in and learn how staying true to your values might just be your most powerful productivity hack.  What We Discuss    [00:02:01] Meet Steven Melman: Clinician, author, mentor    [00:03:59] How to stay present with clientsвАФand leave work at work    [00:06:24] The discipline of mentally вАЬclocking outвАЭ and honoring personal time    [00:08:11] Tackling procrastination: Use reward-based to-do lists    [00:10:38] Creating a goal wheel for a balanced life    [00:13:43] Early career productivity traps and how to avoid them    [00:20:08] Listening over leading: A therapistвАЩs most powerful tool    [00:22:07] The impact of mindful communication in therapy and marriage    [00:24:52] Why humor isnвАЩt just a bonusвАФitвАЩs a strategy    [00:26:58] The future of mental health: Staying productive while staying true    [00:32:48] Where to find StevenвАЩs book and work    Notable Quotes    [00:05:54] вАЬWhen you leave work, you have to mentally clock out. Otherwise, youвАЩre cheating your family and yourself.вАЭ вАФ Steven Melman      [00:10:38] вАЬWeвАЩre implementing self-discipline, overcoming procrastination, and being productive by reviewing our day and planning for tomorrow.вАЭ вАФ Steven Melman      [00:22:07] вАЬYou donвАЩt learn anything when youвАЩre the one doing all the talking. Truly listen and summarize what you hear.вАЭ вАФ Steven Melman      [00:24:52] вАЬI take my work seriouslyвАФbut I use humor to create connection and release tension.вАЭ вАФ Steven Melman    Our Guest  Steven Melman is a licensed psychotherapist, author, and mentor with four decades of clinical experience. His latest book, Guidance for New Clinicians in a Chaotic World, serves as a practical and principled guide for new therapists looking to succeed without sacrificing their personal or spiritual values. title: Tony Khan: Building AEW into a Billion-Dollar Brand and Competing with Wrestling Giants | E106
summary: Tony Khan was captivated by sports and wrestling from a young age. After immersing himself in sports analytics, he joined his fatherвАЩs ventures, using his expertise to build a data-driven team at the Jacksonville Jaguars. Witnessing FulhamвАЩs struggles, Tony saw an opportunity to apply his skills to improve the team and took it. His passion for wrestling ultimately led him to launch All Elite Wrestling (AEW). Despite initial skepticism, TonyвАЩs vision and perseverance allowed AEW to thrive, becoming a major disruptor in the wrestling world. In this episode, Tony joins Ilana to share the power of taking bold actions and seizing opportunities. Tony Khan is the founder, president, and CEO of All Elite Wrestling (AEW), a leading professional wrestling promotion. He also holds executive roles with the Jacksonville Jaguars in the NFL and Fulham F.C. in the English Premier League. With a background in finance and sports analytics, Tony has revolutionized the business of sports entertainment, positioning AEW as a major competitor to WWE. title: Jon Gordon Reveals The Mindset Shift That Builds Winning Teams
summary: Is Your Team Truly CommittedвАФOr Just Chasing Goals? What separates teams that succeed from those that just survive? ItвАЩs not talent. ItвАЩs not resources. ItвАЩs¬†commitment. This week, I sit down with my great friend Jon Gordon, and we got real about what it takes to build a team that doesn't just perform, but thrives under pressure. JonвАЩs new book,¬†The Seven Commitments of a Great Team, is more than theoryвАФitвАЩs the roadmap every leader, entrepreneur, and family builder needs. We talked about why goals arenвАЩt enough. EverybodyвАЩs got goals. But not everybody is¬†committed. Jon broke it down beautifullyвАФcommitment is about action. ItвАЩs about the daily disciplines that align with your vision. Want to make a million dollars this year? That wonвАЩt happen without commitments to how you show up, how you serve, and how you lead your team. вАЬCommitment is going to cost you something,вАЭ Jon said. вАЬYou have to ask yourselfвАФwhat are you willing to give, and what are you willing to give up?вАЭ One of the biggest takeaways? The power of¬†connection¬†and¬†love¬†on a team. That might sound soft, but itвАЩs not. Love is what makes great teams fight for each other. ItвАЩs what transforms a locker room, a boardroom, or a household. вАЬYouвАЩll never have commitment without connection,вАЭ Jon saidвАФand heвАЩs right. If your teamвАЩs not committed to each other, they wonвАЩt be committed to the mission. And when adversity hits, thatвАЩs when the cracks show. We also touched on something every leader needs to hear: emotional fatigue. Carrying the weight of your business, your team, your familyвАФit gets heavy. If you lose your¬†why, you lose your fire. вАЬThe crime isnвАЩt that the passion ran outвАФitвАЩs that you stayed around after it did,вАЭ Jon shared. That line stopped me in my tracks. Leaders, youвАЩve got to reignite your mission or risk slowly fading out. Key Takeaways:    The difference between having goals and living with commitment    Why devotionвАФnot disciplineвАФcreates lasting change    How prayer and purposeful rituals can unlock creative breakthroughs    The role of connection and love in high-performance teams    How to lead through adversity without losing your energy or edge This one hit home for me on a personal level, and I know it will for you too. Go grab¬†The Seven Commitments of a Great Team. It's a game-changer. title: Why We Panic: The Evolutionary Roots of Anxiety and How to Cope Today
summary: In honor of Mental Health Awareness Month, Everyday Better is spotlighting some of the most common, yet least understood, mental health challenges. This week, ABC NewsвАЩ Chief National Correspondent Matt Gutman joins Leah to share his personal journey with anxiety and panic attacks вАУ struggles he hid for nearly two decades, even while reporting live on national television. But in 2020, after a career-altering on-air mistake, Matt was forced to confront his panic head-on, or walk away from the job he loved. In his book вАЬNo Time to Panic,вАЭ Matt explores the science behind anxiety and panic attacks. He explains why humans have panic attacks and how evolution has made anxiety a surprisingly adaptive trait. He also shares the practical tools and mindset shifts that helped him regain control, from breathwork and mindfulness to self-compassion and daily gratitude rituals.¬† Get MattвАЩs book No Time to Panic here. If you liked this episode, youвАЩll also love this one: Conquer Everyday Anxiety with Dr. Wendy Suzuki

title: 449: Breaking Vegan: A Physician's Health Journey from Vegan to Carnivore  with Aimie Apigaian
summary: What happens when a lifelong vegan doctor realizes her "healthy" diet might actually be undermining her health and identity? Dr. Aimie Apigian spent over 30 years believing veganism was the pinnacle of healthy eatingвАФuntil her midlife body had other plans. She shares her dramatic transformation from being a devoted vegan to becoming a full carnivore, challenging everything we think we know about optimal nutrition for women over 40.  Discover why this board-certified physician secretly ate her first fish on vacation, how a collarbone injury revealed decades of suppressed symptoms, and the surprising genetic factor that explained why her plant-based diet wasn't serving her body. Dr. Aimie breaks down the science behind protein absorption, methylation imbalances, and why quality matters more than food category when it comes to midlife nutrition.  This isn't about promoting any single dietвАФit's about becoming your own health detective and learning to listen to what your body truly needs, even when it challenges your deepest beliefs about food and identity.  ¬†  Catch the full episode on YOUTUBE HERE: https://bit.ly/MidlifeConversationsYouTube¬†  ¬†  Learn More About Dr. Aimie Apigaian:¬†  https://www.instagram.com/draimie¬†        Free Gifts for being a listener of Midlife Conversations! title: How To Talk Back To Anxiety with Ethan Kross
podcast_name: Laugh Lines with Kim & Penn Holderness
summary: If youвАЩve been here a while, you know your friend Kim walks with anxiety on a daily basis. I often have thought вАЬif I could sleep through the night, eat enough protein, and manage my anxiety and emotions, IвАЩd be unstoppable.вАЭ And thatвАЩs because anxiety has often stopped me вАФ from resting, from focusing, from showing up the way I want to. My brand of anxiety can be debilitating at times. So how can you begin to manage anxiety and emotions? This week we invited our new friend Ethan Kross on the podcast to help give us tips on just that. Ethan is a psychologist, neuroscientist, and director of the Emotion and Self-Control Lab at the University of Michigan. HeвАЩs also the author of Chatter and Shift, all about taking control of your emotions and the voice in your head. We discuss how distance your self-talk, mentally вАЬtime travelвАЭ and ways Penn deals with emotional flooding due to ADHD. Stick around until the end when we take a call from the Laugh Line on all-inclusive resorts, talk about borgs, and PennвАЩs favorite new show, Game Changer with Sam Reich. title: 364. The Power of Being Shameless, Selfish, and Nosy with Jenny Wood
summary: What if the only thing standing between you and the life you truly want is the courage to begin? In this episode, I speak with Jenny WoodвАФformer Google executive and author of Wild CourageвАФabout what it means to pursue the essential with boldness. title: The Hidden Purpose Behind Your Hardest Seasons with Sarah Jane Wright
summary: In this episode of Lean In With Lizzy, Lizzy sits down with artist, author, and designer Sarah Jane Wright for a profound conversation on loneliness, identity, and how to find beauty in lifeвАЩs wilderness seasons. Sarah Jane opens up about what it means to follow spiritual promptings without a clear path aheadвАФsharing how her move across the country, financial uncertainty, and personal health struggles became sacred ground for transformation. Together, they explore the spiritual symbolism of the wilderness, the difference between holy solitude and hiding, and how women can distinguish GodвАЩs voice from shame. This episode is for anyone who has asked, вАЬWhy now?вАЭ or вАЬWhy me?вАЭвАФand is looking for hope, friendship, and evidence that God is still near. From creative burnout to parenting teenagers, from empty pockets to unexpected callings, Sarah Jane reminds us that we are never too broken or too late to be used by God. title: Dr. Joanna Fortune - The Importance of Play

title: вАЬThis Speech Will Change Your Life!вАЭ COMMENCEMENT SPEECH вАЬWhat Will You Do With The Power That Is YOU?вАЭ Jamie Kern Lima | Columbia University GSB 2025
summary: Imagine if 1 tool, thatвАЩs a simple question to ask yourself each day, can transform your confidence, your self-belief, and your entire life! I am so excited for this episode with you today, as I am sharing the commencement speech I just gave at Columbia University GSB where I share how this one question I ask myself daily has changed everything in my life and it can for you too!  One of the most empowering questions you can ask yourself, that has the potential to help you truly live your best life is this: What will you do, with the power that is, YOU? This question helped me believe that where I came from, and even my past mistakes, didnвАЩt have to determine where I was going. And, along with my faith, helped me believe my dreams were possible, even on the days when it felt like nothing was going my way, or on the days when I was tempted to tell myself lies like I donвАЩt have what it takes, IвАЩm not enough, and big dreams like this donвАЩt happen to people like me.  What will you do with the power that is YOU? ItвАЩs one of the most empowering questions you can ask yourself, because, at itвАЩs core, itвАЩs a reminder that you hold so much power inside of you, and you do! I canвАЩt wait for you to hear this episode and let me know how youвАЩre going to start using this question in your life!  Are You Ready to believe in YOU?рЯЩМ jamiekernlima.comрЯСИ Sign up for my FREE Inspirational Newsletter and get ready for your self-worth to soar!рЯ©Ј¬† ¬†Also, please make sure to take 2 seconds and click the вАЬFollowвАЭ button right here on this page to follow me and the podcast, IвАЩm so grateful and thank you SO much! And whether you're joining me today for yourself or because someone that you love shared this episode with you, I want to welcome you to the Jamie Kern Lima Show podcast family. And remember this episode is not just for you and me. Please share it with every single person that you know because it can change their life too. ItвАЩs such an honor to share this podcast together with you. title: #265 The Art of Comedy | Chip Chantry
summary: Dr. Adam Dorsay, host of the SuperPsyched podcast, welcomes comedian Chip Chantry to discuss the profound role of humor as a coping mechanism and its health benefits. They explore Chip's journey from a fourth grade teacher to an acclaimed comedian, and delve into how laughter improves well-being. The episode also covers the challenges of live comedy, the skill of engaging with audiences, and the invaluable role of humor in therapy and daily life. Both Dr. Dorsay and Chip share anecdotes and insights, making this a compelling episode for anyone interested in psychology, comedy, and personal growth. 00:00 Welcome to SuperPsyched 00:28 The Power of Laughter 01:04 Meet Chip Chantry 01:48 From Teacher to Comedian 03:56 The First Stand-Up Experience 04:58 Balancing Teaching and Comedy 07:26 The Role of Humor in Therapy 13:53 Handling Hecklers and Audience Engagement 21:02 Handling Tough Crowds 21:44 Impromptu Presentation at a Maximum Security Prison 23:13 The Art of Relating to the Audience 24:32 Surviving and Thriving After Bombing on Stage 32:14 Crafting and Finding Comedy 36:58 The Power of Levity in Life 39:14 Conclusion and Final Thoughts Helpful Links: Chip Chantry Chip Chantry YouTube Channel Mayo Clinic Stress Management Article

title: The Secret To a Life Of Fulfilment & Joy
podcast_name: Alcohol-Free Advantage Podcast with Andy Ramage
summary: Hi,Walking the Camino has taught me a great lesson. Less is more. For a long time, I held the conventional view that a life of fulfilment consists of adding more successes, bucket lists, and stuff. But as I walk and read, I am experiencing a very different conclusion. The secret to a great life is to subtract, not add. If you can imagine life as a backpack. When I first started the Camino, my backpack was overflowing with stuff. You quickly figure out every ounce counts when carrying the bag for 8 hours. You learn whatвАЩs essential and what's not. And remove all non-essential items to end up with the vital few. This is the powerful message behind one of my favourite books, Essentialism. As Greg McGowen points out,¬† вАЬThe way of the essentialist is the relentless pursuit of less but better.вАЭ You might find that your life backpack is stuffed full of non-essentials. ThatвАЩs because,¬† вАЬIf you donвАЩt prioritise your life, someone else willвАЭ¬† It is not until we check our life backpack we discover it is full of other people's plans, commitments, and non-essentials. We often pick up these non-essentials without EVER checking to see if they are essential to us. And it is the non-essentials that are weighing you down 24 hours a day. As McGowen suggests: вАЬA non-Essentialist thinks almost everything is importantвАЭ вАЬAn Essentialist thinks almost everything is non-essentialвАЭ¬† Now imagine removing all unnecessary commitments, habits, and workload? How light you would feel. How would you feel if you could put all your life force into the work, causes, and people in your life that are most important? Imagine the joy, meaning and contribution, you would experince if you could focus on things that matter most. In todays podcast I will deep dive into some of the techniques you can use to remove the trival many and discover your vital few! Before I go. What is one commitment or habit you could remove from your life backpack today that will make you feel a little lighter? Your coach¬† Andy PS - Enrolling for July now: If you want to join me on the adventure of a life time.¬† Come and train with me as a coach.¬† It was learning to coach others that transformed my life and now I am on a mission to personally pass on this gift to you. Visit - www.andyramagetraining.com for more information

title: 3 Steps to End a Relationship with Respect
summary: Ending a relationship вАФ whether itвАЩs romantic, professional, or personal вАФ is never easy. But dragging it out or softening the blow too much? ThatвАЩs not kindness, thatвАЩs confusion. In this episode, I break down exactly how to cut ties with clarity and compassion, so you can move on without burning the bridge behind you. This episode is sponsored by Cozy Earth. title: Episode 180: Ў®ЎІ ўИЎІўДЎѓџМўЖ Ў≥ўЕџМ ЏЖџМ Џ©ЎІЎ± Џ©ўЖџМўЕЎЯ
summary: ЎІўЊџМЎ≤ўИЎѓ џ±џЄџ∞ ўЊЎІЎѓЏ©Ў≥Ў™ "ўДЎІўЕ Ў™ЎІ Џ©ўДЎІўЕ" : Ў®ЎІ ўИЎІўДЎѓџМўЖ Ў≥ўЕџМ ЏЖџМ Џ©ЎІЎ± Џ©ўЖџМўЕЎЯ¬†Ў™ўИ ЎІџМўЖ ЎІўЊџМЎ≤ўИЎѓ ўЕџМвАМЎЃўИЎІўЕ ўВЎµўЗ џМЏ© Ў≤ўЖ ўИ ўЕЎ±Ўѓ Ў±ўИ Ў®Ў±ЎІЎ™ўИўЖ Ў®ЏѓўЕ Џ©ўЗ Ў®ЎІ ЎєЎіўВ џМЎІ ЎіЎІџМЎѓ ўЗўЕ џМЏ©ўЕ ўБЎіЎІЎ± Ў≥ўЖЎ™ ЎІЎ≤ЎѓўИЎІЎђ Џ©Ў±ЎѓўЖЎѓЎМ Ў®ЎєЎѓ Ў™ЎµўЕџМўЕ ЏѓЎ±ўБЎ™ўЖЎѓ Џ©ўЗ Ў®ЏЖўЗвАМЎѓЎІЎ± Ў®ЎіўЖ ЏЖўИўЖ ўЕџМвАМЎЃўИЎІЎ≥Ў™ўЖЎѓ ўЊЎІџМўЗвАМўЗЎІџМ ЎЃЎІўЖўИЎІЎѓўЗ Ў±ўИ ўЕЎ≠Џ©ўЕ ўЖЏѓўЗ ЎѓЎІЎ±ўЖЎѓ џМЎІ ЏЖўИўЖ ўИЎІўВЎєЎІ ЎѓўИЎ≥Ў™ ЎѓЎІЎіЎ™ўЖЎѓ Ў®ЏЖўЗ ЎѓЎІЎ± Ў®ЎіўЖ. Ў®Ў±ЎІџМ ЎІўИўЖ Ў®ЏЖўЗ Ў≥ўВўБ Ў≥ЎІЎЃЎ™ўЖЎѓЎМ ўДЎ®ЎІЎ≥ ЎЃЎ±џМЎѓўЖЎѓЎМ ЎЇЎ∞ЎІџМ ЏѓЎ±ўЕ ўБЎ±ЎІўЗўЕ Џ©Ў±ЎѓўЖЎѓ ўИ Ў®ўЗ ўЕЎѓЎ±Ў≥ўЗ ЎЃўИЎ®џМ ўБЎ±Ў≥Ў™ЎІЎѓўЖЎі. ЎҐџМЎІ Ў®ўЗ ЎІџМўЖ Ў≤ўЖ ўИ ўЕЎ±Ўѓ Ў®ЎІџМЎѓ ЏѓўБЎ™ "ўЊЎѓЎ± ўИ ўЕЎІЎѓЎ±"ЎЯ ЎҐџМЎІ ўБўВЎЈ Ў®ўЗ ЎѓўЖџМЎІ ЎҐўИЎ±ЎѓўЖ џМЏ© Ў®ЏЖўЗ ўИ ўБЎ±ЎІўЗўЕ Џ©Ў±ЎѓўЖ ўЕЎІџМЎ≠Ў™ЎІЎђ Ў®Ў±ЎІџМ ўЊЎѓЎ± ўИ ўЕЎІЎѓЎ± ЎіЎѓўЖ Џ©ЎІўБџМўЗЎЯ џМЎІ Ў®ЎІџМЎѓ Ў®ўЗ ЏЖџМЎ≤ўЗЎІџМ ЎєўЕџМўВвАМЎ™Ў±ЎМ ЎІўЖЎ≥ЎІўЖџМвАМЎ™Ў± ўИ ЎєЎІЎіўВЎІўЖўЗвАМЎ™Ў±џМ ўБЏ©Ў± Џ©Ў±ЎѓЎЯ¬† ўЖЎІЏѓўБЎ™ўЗвАМўЗЎІџМ ЏЖЎІўДЎі ўЗЎІџМ Ў±ўИЎ≤ЎІўЖўЗ ўЕЎІ ЎѓЎ± Ў±ўИЎІЎ®ЎЈ¬† ўИ ЎІЎ™ўБЎІўВЎІЎ™ Ў≤ўЖЎѓЏѓџМ¬† Ў®ЎІ¬† Ў≤Ў®ЎІўЖ Ў≥ЎІЎѓўЗ ўИ ЎєЎІўЕџМЎІўЖўЗ Ў™ўИЎ≥ЎЈ Ў≥ўИўДўЕЎІЎ≤ Ў®Ў±ўВЏѓџМЎ±ЎМ ўДЎІџМўБ Џ©ўИЏЖ ўИ ЎІЎ≥Ў™Ў±ЎІЎ™ЏШџМЎ≥Ў™ Ў±ўИЎІЎ®ЎЈ ўЕўИЎЂЎ±ЎМ Ў±ўИЎІўЖ ЎѓЎ±ўЕЎІўЖЏѓЎ± ЎѓЎ± Ў™ўИЎ±ўЖЎ™ўИ Џ©ЎІўЖЎІЎѓЎІ ЎІўЊџМЎ≤ўИЎѓ џ±џЈџі Ў®ЎєЎѓ ЎІЎ≤ ўЕЎ±Ў≤ЏѓЎ∞ЎІЎ±џМ ЏЖўЗ Џ©ўЖџМўЕЎЯЏ©ЎІўЖЎІўД Ў™ўДЏѓЎ±ЎІўЕ Ў≥ўИўДўЕЎІЎ≤ Ў®Ў±ўВЏѓџМЎ± Ў≥ўБЎІЎ±Ўі Џ©Ў™ЎІЎ® "ўЖўВЎі ЎѓЎ±ўИўЖ" ўИЎ±Џ©ЎіЎІўЊ вАЬЎ™Џ©ўЖџМЏ© ўЗЎІџМ ўЕЎ±Ў≤ЏѓЎ∞ЎІЎ±џМвАЭ¬† ЎѓўИЎ±ўЗвАМ ¬ЂўЕЎєЎђЎ≤ўЗвАМ ўВўДўЕ ўИ Џ©ЎІЎЇЎ∞ Ў®ЎІ ўЕЎѓџМЎ™џМЎіўЖ Ў®Ў±ЎІџМ Ў±ўЗЎІџМџМ ЎІЎ≤ ЎІЎ≥ЎІЎ±Ў™ Ў™Ў±Ў≥¬ї ЎѓЎ±џМЎІўБЎ™ ЎѓўИЎ±ўЗ "Ў±ўЗЎІџМџМ ЎІЎ≤ ўЗџМўИўДЎІџМ ЎІЎ≥Ў™Ў±Ў≥ ўИ ўЖЏѓЎ±ЎІўЖџМ" ЎѓЎ±џМЎІўБЎ™ ЎѓўИЎ±ўЗ ЎЃўИЎѓ ўЕЎ≠Џ©ўЕ ЎѓЎ± ўЗўБЎ™ ўВЎѓўЕ ЎѓЎ±џМЎІўБЎ™ ЎѓўИЎ±ўЗ ўЗўБЎ™ Ў≥ўЖЏѓ Ў®ўЖЎІџМ Ў±ЎІЎ®ЎЈўЗ ўЕўИЎЂЎ± Lam Ta Kalam is a Well-received Farsi Podcast Episode 180: How to Deal with Toxic Parents? title: SET Boundaries and MASTER Your Emotions for Success!
summary: Want to achieve success and master your emotions? Setting boundaries is the key!  In this episode, host Lisa Urbanski sits down with author and speaker Sagar Constantin to dive into the crucial topics of personal boundaries, emotional management, and the challenges of balancing commitments, such as Sagar's PhD work and social media engagement.¬† Through their engaging discussion, they explore effective strategies for setting and maintaining boundaries, highlighting the significance of honesty, self-respect, and the importance of embracing emotions rather than suppressing them. Learn how to prioritize your emotional well-being, stop people-pleasing, start living a life that TRULY aligns with your values and goals, and discover the power of healthy boundaries to take the first step towards a more fulfilling life. рЯФЧ Explore Sagar Constantin's empowering insights on self-development at¬†sagarconstantin.com. рЯУ± Connect with Sagar Constantin on:¬† Facebook:¬†https://www.facebook.com/SagarConstantinAuthor Instagram:¬†https://www.instagram.com/sagar.constantin.author/ рЯУЦ¬†Discover Sagar Constantin's empowering insights on self-discovery and personal growth by purchasing her book, "Be Your Own Rock,"¬†here. title: 4. Unlicensed Therapy With Dave & Marshall
summary: Ever trauma bonded over a laugh? In this episode, Dave and Marshall spill it all вАФ toxic relationships, emotional healing, narcissists, family court chaos, and why crying in a truck is perfectly normal. ItвАЩs deep, itвАЩs raw, and itвАЩs got more swearing than a highway CB. But underneath the banter is a serious message: heal your wounds, own your truth, and donвАЩt wear a headset unless youвАЩre 80. YouвАЩll laugh, nod, maybe cry вАФ or at least feel better about your own life choices.

title: Navigating Health Care Systems & Getting Positive Outcomes With Lucy Proud
summary: In episode 98 of The Road to Wisdom Podcast Chloe and Keshia speak to friend, Lucy Proud who shares her personal experience with the medical system following a paralysis tick bite her toddler was hospitalised for. The story focusing on the challenges and fears associated with medical interventions, particularly in the context of motherhood. Lucy shares a profound story about the lessons learned about intuition, surrender, and navigating the complexities of healthcare. The discussion emphasizes the importance of empowerment, trust, and understanding within the medical system, as well as the need for parents to advocate for their children's health. In this conversation, Keshia & Chloe share their experiences navigating the medical system while caring for ¬†young children who faced serious health challenges. She discusses the importance of co-creating relationships with medical staff, advocating for her child's needs, and the role of nutrition in recovery. Lucy emphasizes the power of parental intuition and knowledge in ensuring the best outcomes for children in medical settings. Lucy can be found at @lucyproud Loved what you heard in this episode? Your support means the world. Make sure to hit that subscribe button, spread the word with your pals, and drop us a review. title: Reverse Diabetes, PCOS & Blood Pressure Through Food | Dr. Suman Agarwal on Health & Nutrition
summary: рЯСЗ About the guest Suman Agarwal is a leading nutritionist and fitness expert, certified by Oxford, who has spent over 24 years helping more than 20,000 people, including celebrities and business leaders, achieve healthier lifestyles. рЯСЗ Highlights рЯ•¶ Diet for PCOS: PCOS is prevalent in 10-25% of women in India, and addressing it requires a focus on balanced nutrition, exercise, and lifestyle changes rather than just medication. рЯТ™ Weight Training for Diabetics: Diabetics should engage in weight training to prevent muscle loss and better manage blood sugar levels, as maintaining muscle mass helps in glucose metabolism. вП∞ Intermittent Fasting Misconceptions: Long fasting periods (16 hours) may lead to muscle loss and other health issues, especially for menopausal women; a 12-14 hour fasting window is recommended instead. рЯНП Fruits and Diabetes: Many diabetics mistakenly believe fruits are safe; however, consuming them on an empty stomach can spike blood sugar levels, and certain fruits should be limited. рЯ•Ы Calcium and Blood Pressure: Low vitamin D and calcium absorption can contribute to high blood pressure, and incorporating yogurt into the diet can help manage it. рЯМ± Balanced Nutrition: A balanced intake of proteins, fats, and carbohydrates is crucial for overall health, especially for those with chronic conditions. рЯТ§ Sleep Hygiene: Proper sleep hygiene is essential for maintaining immunity and overall health; avoiding screens before bedtime is advised. рЯПЛпЄПвАНвЩАпЄП Exercise Importance: Regular exercise, including weight training and cardio, is vital for managing chronic conditions and improving overall health. рЯСЗTimestamps [00:00] - Introduction [02:32] - Muscle recovery becomes challenging after age 14 [07:18] - Vegetarian diets lack essential fatty acids [10:02] - Key factors for PCOS. title: EP. 8: Bandaids vs. Breakthroughs | The Critical Difference in ADHD Management That Changes Everything | ADHD with Jenna Free
summary: You can get your new and improved free ADHD Reset Guide here - adhdwithjennafree.com/adhdguide  Chapters  00:06 Understanding ADHD Approaches  04:56 The Band-Aid Approach vs. Transformation  14:19 Information Overload and Implementation  23:05 The Three Types of Regulation  Summary  In this episode, I discuss two primary approaches to managing ADHD: the band-aid approach, which focuses on quick fixes, and the transformational approach, which emphasizes internal work and awareness. I highlight the importance of understanding dysregulation and its impact on the effectiveness of ADHD management tools (yes even using calendars can get better with regulation).  Takeaways   The band-aid approach offers quick fixes but lacks long-term effectiveness. Transformation requires internal work and awareness, not just external tools. Dysregulation can hinder the effectiveness of ADHD management tools. Information hoarding can lead to inaction and frustration. Effective ADHD management combines both external tools and internal transformation. Understanding the three types of regulation is crucial for managing ADHD. Nervous system regulation alone is not enough for lasting change. Transformation takes time and patience, not instant solutions.  Connect with Jenna   Instagram TikTok

title: Stop Chasing Happiness: Why YouвАЩre Never Satisfied (and How to Fix It) | Clutterbug Podcast # 274
podcast_name: ClutterBug - Organize, Clean and Transform your Home & Life
summary: If youвАЩve ever found yourself saying, вАЬIвАЩll be happy whenвА¶вАЭ вАФ when the house is clean, when the kids are older, when you finally get that promotion or dream kitchen вАФ youвАЩre not alone. Maybe youвАЩre always chasing the next goal, the next purchase, the next big thingвА¶ but somehow, happiness still feels out of reach. In this episode, IвАЩm getting real about something IвАЩve been struggling with for years: destination addiction. ItвАЩs that constant feeling of needing more to finally feel content. And spoiler alert вАФ itвАЩs not just a mindset issueвА¶ itвАЩs biology. ItвАЩs what psychologists call the hedonic treadmill, and friends, IвАЩve been sprinting on it for most of my adult life. So today, IвАЩm hitting pause. IвАЩm sharing the science behind why we adapt so quickly to the good stuff, why our happiness resets to вАЬmeh,вАЭ and what we can actually do about it. If youвАЩve been feeling chronically dissatisfied, overwhelmed, or like youвАЩre missing the joy in your already-amazing life вАФ this oneвАЩs for you. Whether youвАЩre in full hustle mode or feeling a little lost between вАЬnext big things,вАЭ this episode is your permission slip to slow down, breathe, and start appreciating the incredible life youвАЩre living right now. вЬ® In this episode, I talk about: What the вАЬhedonic treadmillвАЭ really means вАФ and how it keeps us stuck How society, social media, and even ADHD feed into our addiction to вАЬmoreвАЭ The emotional crash that comes after a big milestone Why midlife crises arenвАЩt what we think they are How to raise your вАЬbaselineвАЭ and feel truly content again Simple but powerful ways to practice gratitude and presence every day рЯТЫ This isnвАЩt about giving up on dreams. ItвАЩs about learning to love the life youвАЩve already built. рЯСЗ Mentioned in this episode: Here are the articles and resources I talked about вАФ вЖТ What Is the Hedonic Treadmill? title: Together Miniseries #3: The Birth of Authentic Power
summary: In this episode of The Gary Zukav Podcast, Gary continues the Together Miniseries by exploring the profound transformation from external power to authentic power. As humanity collectively steps into a new consciousness, Gary discusses how aligning your personality with your soul becomes the new foundation of power. He invites listeners to recognize that true strength lies not in control or domination, but in the experience of love, compassion, and inner wisdom. This episode takes a deeper look into the outdated model of power based on manipulation and controlвАФwhat Gary calls "external power"вАФand how it continues to shape global institutions, relationships, and individual identities. He explains why this model is not only destructive but obsolete, and how the frightened parts of our personality resist the shift toward love. Through rich metaphors and reflections, Gary challenges us to examine how our inner perceptions shape our experience of the world. The solution, Gary emphasizes, is to cultivate authentic powerвАФa power that comes from within, born of love rather than fear. This new consciousness asks us to embrace harmony, cooperation, sharing, and reverence for life. As we learn to identify the parts of ourselves motivated by fear versus those motivated by love, we open the doorway to lasting transformation. Please do not take anything that Gary or Linda share as true just because they say it. If it resonates with you, experiment with it in your life. If it doesn't, let it go. What YouвАЩll Discover in This Episode: (01:02) вАФ Why external power is not only outdated but actively obstructs spiritual growth (04:10) вАФ How frightened parts of our personality interpret love as weakness and distort our worldview (06:45) вАФ What it means to see the world through a mirror of perception and how it impacts your reality (10:05) вАФ The difference between happiness and joy, and why joy is the foundation of authentic power (13:55) вАФ Why change doesnвАЩt happen through influence or wealth but only through inner transformation (18:15) вАФ A powerful journaling practice to identify when you're acting from fear vs. love Learn how to align your personality with your soul and create authentic power in your life. The Authentic Power Guidelines will guide you on this powerful journey. Learn More Here. Embrace the Soul Step Challenge: This week, look back on moments when you sought validation, success, or control from the outside world. Ask yourself: Was this coming from fear or love? Take time to journal your emotional experiences around these moments. Did you feel a temporary highвАФor a lasting sense of inner peace? Use this practice to become more aware of the frightened parts of your personalityвАФand choose instead to act from the loving parts that lead to authentic power. One step, one choice at a time. title: Heather Mitts Feeley: Embracing the Gold Medal Mindset for Success in Life and Leadership
summary: Heather Mitts Feeley is a three-time Olympic gold medalist and former U.S. WomenвАЩs National Soccer Team defender, celebrated for her relentless grit, tenacity, and leadership both on and off the field. A University of Florida alum, Heather helped build their soccer program from the ground up and became a national champion. After retiring from soccer, she transformed her athleteвАЩs mindset into motivational speaking and advocacy, inspiring business leaders and championing equity in sports. Today, Heather balances her passion for empowering others with her most cherished role: being a present and loving mom to her three children.  Takeaways:  Mindset Matters Everywhere: The вАШgold medal mindsetвАЩ isnвАЩt just for athletes; Heather shares how the principles of resilience, goal setting, and controlling what you can translate seamlessly into business and life.  Surround Yourself with the Right People: Heather emphasizes the importance of curating your inner circle, noting that the company you keep can fuelвАФor hinderвАФyour growth and dreams.  Adversity Builds Strength: From career-altering injuries to personal loss, HeatherвАЩs journey reveals that hardship can sharpen your toughness, shape your character, and lead to your greatest moments of triumph.  ¬†Sound Bites:  вАЬYou retire from the sport; you donвАЩt retire from being an athlete. The dog mentality is still there.вАЭ  вАЬWe are the sum of the five people that we spend the most time with. That blows my mind, because at one point in my life, my five werenвАЩt great, but now they are amazing.вАЭ  вАЬGiving yourself permission to imagine a positive outcomeвАФdreamingвАФdoesnвАЩt stop when you grow up, itвАЩs something we need all our lives.вАЭ  MickвАЩs Quote:  вАЬA great leader knows their team better than their team knows themselves. Meaning you know how they think, you know the right positions to put them in, and you set them up for success by knowing them better than they know themselves.вАЭ  ¬†Connect & Discover Heather:  LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/heather-mitts-1b50a18/  Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/hmitts2/?hl=en  Website: Players for Good  FOLLOW MICK ON:Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mickunplugged/ ¬†Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/mickunplugged/ ¬†YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@mickunplugged ¬†LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mickhunt/Website: https://www.mickhuntofficial.com¬†¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/mick-unplugged/ See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Raising Kids, Staying Present: Fatherhood in a Digital World | With Amit Kalley
summary: In this episode of the Feel Good for Men Who Want Change podcast, host Tim Barber and guest Amit Kalley explore what it means to be a father in the digital age. Amit, a former teacher who founded For Working Parents, an organisation that coaches schools and parents on how to navigate the digital world safely. He shares his personal journey from the classroom to coaching, highlighting the unique challenges he faced and the insights he gained along the way. AmitвАЩs experiences provide a rich backdrop for discussing the evolving role of fathers in a world increasingly dominated by technology. How does screen time affect children, and what role do fathers play in managing it? Amit offers valuable insights into the parenting challenges associated with the digital age, particularly focusing on the impact of screen time on children. He discusses the delicate balance parents must strike between allowing their children to engage with technology and ensuring it does not negatively affect their development. Amit emphasises the critical role fathers play in this dynamic, advocating for their active involvement in setting boundaries and guiding their childrenвАЩs interactions with digital devices. Why is it important for fathers to engage meaningfully in their childrenвАЩs lives? The discussion highlights the significance of fathers taking an active and intentional role in their childrenвАЩs upbringing. Amit stresses the importance of fathers being present and engaged, not just physically but emotionally and mentally too. He talks about the need to balance cultural values with modern parenting practices, ensuring that children receive a well-rounded upbringing that respects tradition while embracing innovation. Amit believes meaningful engagement is crucial for building strong, lasting relationships with children and fostering their overall well-being. How can fathers manage digital influences while maintaining open communication with their children? Both Amit and Tim emphasise the necessity of open communication in the family, he advocates for setting clear expectations and boundaries around technology use, encouraging fathers to have honest conversations with their children about the benefits and potential pitfalls of digital media. By following these steps fathers can help their children develop a healthy relationship with technology.What does it mean for fathers to embrace vulnerability and authenticity in their parenting roles? The episode encourages dads to be genuine and open in how they relate to their children. Amit shares how vulnerability helps build deeper connections and creates a supportive home environment. His story is a reminder that confidence and compassion go hand-in-hand in modern fatherhood. So after listening to this episode, why not reflect on your own digital habits, and how they might be impacting your childrenвАЩs relationship with screens. title: Redefining Masculinity: What It Means to Be a Good Man | With Jon Alexander
summary: In this episode of the Feel Good for Men Who Want Change podcast, host Tim Barber and guest, Jon Alexander, embark on a deep dive into the evolving concept of masculinity, examining how it intersects with purpose and personal agency. Jon, known for his insightful book "Citizens" and as the co-founder of the New Citizen Project, shares his transformative journey. He recounts his path from the competitive world of sports, where traditional notions of masculinity often dominate, to a career in advertising, and ultimately now focussing on citizenship and societal contribution. This journey reflects a broader shift in understanding what it means to be a man in contemporary society, moving away from outdated stereotypes and towards a more inclusive and purposeful identity. How does consumerism shape our identities and mental health? The discussion between Tim and Jon delves into the pervasive impact of consumerism on how individuals perceive themselves and their mental well-being. They explore the idea that a consumer-driven mindset can lead to a fragmented sense of self, where personal worth is often measured by material possessions and superficial achievements. Jon emphasises the need to transition from this consumer mindset to one that prioritizes community and contribution. By focusing on what we can give rather than what we can acquire, individuals can cultivate a more fulfilling and mentally healthy life. This shift not only benefits personal well-being but also fosters a sense of belonging and purpose within the community. Why is local action and community involvement crucial for redefining men's roles? Jon highlights the significance of engaging in local action and becoming actively involved in community initiatives as a way for men to redefine their roles in society. He argues that by participating in community projects and contributing to the common good, men can break free from the constraints of traditional masculinity and find new ways to express their identities. This involvement encourages men to engage meaningfully with those around them, fostering connections and building a sense of purpose that transcends individual achievements. By redefining their roles through community engagement, men can contribute to a more equitable and supportive society, where everyone has the opportunity to thrive. So, after everything you've heard in this episode, how can you start showing up more meaningfully; for yourself, your community, and the people around you? title: Sosa Henkoma From Trauma to Triumph: The Journey of Resilience
summary: In this episode of "Getting There," Brogan welcomes Sosa, a remarkable individual whose journey through unimaginable hardships has transformed into a story of resilience and purpose. Sosa shares his experiences of being abused by his stepmother, placed into care at just eight years old, and later exploited by gangs. Despite these adversities, he has harnessed his pain to shed light on the dark realities of child grooming and to inspire change within communities and for younger generations. This conversation is a powerful reminder of the strength of the human spirit and the importance of giving back. Join us for an honest and inspiring discussion that serves as a wake-up call for all.

title: Peak Somatic Experiences, Complex Trauma, and the Nervous System
summary: What happens when your body feels more than your mind can make sense of?In this deeply grounding episode of Trauma Rewired, Elisabeth and Jennifer open up a necessary and often overlooked conversation about peak somatic experiencesвАФpowerful moments of healing that happen in the body, not the mind.  Whether youвАЩre drawn to psychedelics, breathwork, or trauma release exercises, this episode unpacks the neurobiological, emotional, and spiritual layers of somatic breakthroughs. But more importantly, itвАЩs a cautionary tale: for those with chronic dissociation or unresolved trauma, these experiences can be overwhelming or even destabilizing if approached without preparation, interoceptive training, and long-term integration.  Through the lens of Neurosomatic Intelligence (NSI), youвАЩll learn how peak somatic experiences interact with neuroplasticity, the default mode network, implicit memory, and emotional patterning. Jennifer and Elisabeth share their personal journeys and offer practical tools to build capacity, safety, and support beforeвАФand afterвАФthese experiences.  Whether youвАЩve already walked this path or are considering it, this episode will help you explore the power and risks of embodiment with reverence, curiosity, and care.  Topics discussed in this episode:   What defines a peak somatic experience through the NSI lens      The science behind psychedelics, neuroplasticity, and the 5HT2A receptor      Why preparation is more than a checklistвАФitвАЩs a relationship with your body      The role of the default mode network in identity, emotion, and trauma      Emotional breakthroughs: what they are and what theyвАЩre not      How dissociation impacts your ability to benefit from embodiment work      Why "purging" trauma isn't the goalвАФand what to do instead      The risks of somatic experiences for trauma survivors without proper resourcing      Real-life stories of dysregulation and recovery after peak experiences      Building interoceptive awareness and emotional processing capacity      Why integration is the true work of healingвАФnot the experience itself      Rethinking psychedelics as collective healing, not just personal growth      ¬†  Get started training your nervous system with our FREE 2-week offer on the Brain Based Membership site: https://www.rewiretrial.com  Connect with us on social media: @trauma.rewired  Join our Trauma Rewired Facebook Group! https://www.facebook.com/groups/761101225132846  FREE 1 Year Supply of Vitamin D + 5 Travel Packs from Athletic Greens when you use my exclusive offer: https://www.drinkag1.com/rewired  Trauma Rewired podcast¬† is intended to educate and inform but does not constitute medical, psychological or other professional advice or services. title: ATG 230: Leaders Who Can't Be Questioned End Up Doing Questionable Things
podcast_name: All It Takes Is A Goal
summary: Want to know the simplest leadership principle that makes people elbow each other when I share it on stage? "Leaders who can't be questioned end up doing questionable things." In this episode, I'll share how the gap between the boardroom and the break room creates problems, why your team might be editing their feedback by 15%, and my embarrassing story about a CEO with terrible Zoom lighting (spoiler: everyone lied and said it looked great). Whether you're leading a company, a family, or just yourself, I'll show you how to create an environment where honest feedback thrives instead of dies. Because the higher you climb on the success ladder, the more opportunities you'll have to behave poorly, and the more you need people brave enough to tell you the truth. title: Understanding This Will Change How You Experience Your Entire Life

Her groundbreaking research proves that every human being is biologically hardwired for spirituality вАФ even if, right now, it means nothing to you.¬† Dr. MillerвАЩs work shows that spiritual connection вАФ however you define it вАФ changes your brain for the better.¬† It can protect you from depression, anxiety, addiction, hopelessness, and burnout, and it gives you powerful tools to get through the hardest times in your life.¬† We are living in a moment where believing in something beyond yourself can feel impossible.¬† You might be feeling uncertain, anxious, or even pessimistic about the future вАФ but this conversation will open your mind to possibilities you may have never considered before.¬† Dr. Miller will prove to you, using science, that when you awaken this innate part of your brain, you unlock resilience, clarity, connection, and a deep sense of purpose.¬† YouвАЩll learn:¬† -The #1 thing that separates people who are resilient and hopeful from those who struggle¬† -The enormous difference between spirituality and religion (and why that matters for your mental health)¬† -How to unlock your brainвАЩs вАЬawakenedвАЭ network вАФ the key to creativity, intuition, and deep meaning¬† -How to nurture spirituality in yourself and your kids (even if you donвАЩt know where to start)¬† -Why crises, heartbreak, and hardship can be portals to spiritual growth and personal transformation. Whether youвАЩre deeply spiritual, skeptical, or somewhere in between, this episode will inspire you to see that spirituality isnвАЩt just a вАЬnice to haveвАЭ вАФ itвАЩs an essential part of being human.¬† If youвАЩre feeling stuck, lost, hopeless, or like there must be something more to life, this conversation is coming to you at exactly the right moment.¬† For more resources, click here for the podcast episode page.¬† If you liked this episode, youвАЩll love listening to this one next: How to Find Your Purpose & Design the Life You Want Connect with Mel:¬† ¬† Get MelвАЩs #1 bestselling book, The Let Them TheoryWatch the episodes on YouTubeFollow Mel on Instagram¬†The Mel Robbins Podcast InstagramMel's TikTok¬†Sign up for MelвАЩs personal letter¬†Subscribe to SiriusXM Podcasts+ to listen to new episodes ad-freeDisclaimer
